Name: Azerbaijan Data Sovereignty & Cloud Security Framework
Description: Compliance framework for cloud workloads operated under Azerbaijani jurisdiction. Aggregates technical controls derived from: (1) Law on Personal Data No. 998-IIIQ (2010); (2) Law on Information, Informatization and Information Protection No. 460-IQ (1998); (3) Decree on the National Cybersecurity Strategy of the Republic of Azerbaijan; (4) Regulations on the Operation of State Information Systems and Data Centers (Azintelecom). The framework targets state ministries, critical infrastructure, and Azintelecom-hosted workloads to enforce data residency, encryption, audit, and access governance requirements inside Azerbaijani sovereign cloud zones.

Requirements.Name: Data Residency and Geographic Restriction
Requirements.Description: All personal data and state-classified information must be processed and stored within infrastructure physically located in the Republic of Azerbaijan. Cross-border data transfer requires explicit authorization from the State Service for Special Communications and Information Security. Cloud workloads MUST restrict region usage to authorized sovereign zones.

Requirements.Name: Encryption of Personal Data at Rest
Requirements.Description: Şəxsi məlumatlar haqqında Qanun, Maddə 7 — Personal data must be protected against unauthorized access through cryptographic means. All data stores holding personal or state-classified information SHALL be encrypted at rest using customer-managed keys.

Requirements.Name: Encryption of Data in Transit
Requirements.Description: Information transmitted between systems or to data subjects MUST be protected from interception. All public-facing endpoints SHALL enforce TLS 1.2 or higher and reject plain-HTTP traffic.

Requirements.Name: Identity and Access Governance
Requirements.Description: Access to state information systems must be authenticated, authorized, logged, and reviewed. Privileged accounts SHALL use multi-factor authentication. Inactive credentials MUST be rotated or revoked. Root accounts MUST NOT be used for routine operations.

Requirements.Name: Continuous Auditing and Logging
Requirements.Description: İnformasiya, informasiyalaşdırma və informasiyanın mühafizəsi haqqında Qanun, Maddə 12 — Operations on state information systems must be auditable. CloudTrail or equivalent logging SHALL be enabled across all regions, log integrity SHALL be validated, and logs SHALL be retained per the National Archives retention schedule.

Requirements.Name: Network Perimeter Hardening
Requirements.Description: Cloud network perimeters serving state workloads MUST restrict ingress to required ports only. Direct exposure of administrative protocols (SSH, RDP) to the public Internet is PROHIBITED for production workloads.

Requirements.Name: Threat Detection and Incident Response
Requirements.Description: National Cybersecurity Strategy, Section 4.3 — Critical information infrastructure operators SHALL deploy continuous threat detection and report incidents to the National CERT (CERT.GOV.AZ) within 24 hours of discovery.

Requirements.Name: Backup and Disaster Recovery
Requirements.Description: Operators of state information systems SHALL maintain backups sufficient to restore service in compliance with Azintelecom's continuity-of-government RPO/RTO targets. Backups MUST be stored in a separate availability zone within the sovereign region.

Requirements.Name: Secret and Credential Hygiene
Requirements.Description: Hard-coded credentials, API keys, and secrets in application configuration are PROHIBITED. Secrets SHALL be managed through a centralized secrets store with automated rotation.

Requirements.Name: Vulnerability and Configuration Management
Requirements.Description: Operators SHALL continuously scan workloads for known vulnerabilities and remediate critical findings within 30 days. Container images and AMIs MUST be scanned before production deployment.
